heading: Department of Defense
label: 3005 Department of Defense
text: Except to the extent inconsistent with the provisions of this chapter or other provisions of law, the provisions of title 5 shall be applicable to the Department of Defense.
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , known as the National Security Act of 1947, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.act July 26, 1947, ch. 34361 Stat. 495

Codification, which directed the general amendment of section 201 of , was executed by adding a new section 201 to the Act and classifying it to this section, in light of the repeal of former section 201 by , , .Section 6742(b)(6) of Pub. Prior ProvisionsA prior section 3005, , as added , , which applied the provisions of title 4 of the Revised Statutes to the Department of Defense, except to the extent inconsistent with the provisions of this chapter, was technically repealed by , , , but continued to be set out as this section prior to the enactment of .act July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title II, § 201(d)Aug. 10, 1949, ch. 412, § 463 Stat. 579Pub. L. 87–651, § 30776 Stat. 526Pub. L. 116–92Sept. 7, 1962A prior section 201 of , as added , , which established the Department of Defense as an Executive Department and established within it the various military departments, consisted of subsecs. (a) and (b), classified to section 171 of former Title 5, Executive Departments and Government Officers and Employees; subsec. (c), classified to section 1 of former Title 5; and subsec. (d), classified first to section 171–1 of former Title 5, then to , and finally to this section (see note above).
